title: Purged walk-forward OOF stacking with sigmoid calibration
summary: Because the label is a one-lap-forward event, nearby samples are leakage-prone; the strongest ensemble candidate is to generate strictly chronological OOF predictions from heterogeneous models and stack them with a rank-preserving calibrator rather than calibrating in-sample or using isotonic ties.
rationale: Because the label is a one-lap-forward event, nearby samples are leakage-prone; the strongest ensemble candidate is to generate strictly chronological OOF predictions from heterogeneous models and stack them with a rank-preserving calibrator rather than calibrating in-sample or using isotonic ties.
implementation_hint: Build OOF predictions from race-ordered folds with a small embargo/purge around fold boundaries, train 2-3 diverse base models (for example current GBDT, CatBoost, pseudo-horizon model), then fit a logistic/sigmoid stacker on OOF scores only; prefer sigmoid over isotonic if preserving ROC-AUC ranking is the priority.
expected_effect: Better leaderboard transfer than single-model tuning, with reduced CV optimism and better probability stability.
risk: More moving parts; if fold design is weak, the stacker will overfit the same leakage the ensemble is meant to avoid.
